scope: Just enough theory to choose a strategy — inspection precedes automation.
key_concepts: Understanding why data is missing is a prerequisite to handling it.
Missing Completely At Random (MCAR): Missingness is independent of any variable (observed or unobserved).
Missing At Random (MAR): Missingness depends only on observed variables, not on the missing values themselves.
Missing Not At Random (MNAR): Missingness depends on the unobserved value itself.
Diagnostics and inspection must always precede automated imputation strategies.

definitions.definition: Missingness has no relationship with any data.
Missingness can be explained by other observed variables in the dataset.
Missingness is related to the value that is missing.

examples: MCAR: A sensor drops a reading because of a random power glitch.
MAR: Men are less likely to report their weight than women (missingness in weight depends on the observed gender variable).
MNAR: People with very high incomes are less likely to report their income (missingness depends on the income itself).
exclusions: Mathematical proofs of Rubin's missing data theory.
Complex statistical tests for MCAR (Little's test) - keep it intuitive.
